title: Shared definitions for blemeesd/2 wire frames
description: Reusable $defs referenced by the per-frame schemas. Not a frame schema itself.

SessionId.description: Client-owned session identifier. The daemon does not validate format at the protocol layer, but clients must supply a UUID (e.g. str(uuid.uuid4())) — the Claude backend forwards it verbatim to `claude -p --session-id`, which only accepts UUIDs. Codex uses its own threadId internally; for backend-neutrality always pass a UUID.

RequestId.description: Opaque correlation id set by the client on request frames; echoed on the reply.

Seq.description: Monotonic per-session sequence number injected by the daemon on session-stream frames.

PeerPid.description: Peer PID captured via SO_PEERCRED; may be absent on platforms that do not expose it.

Backend.description: Backend selector. Set on inbound blemeesd.open and echoed on every outbound agent.* frame so clients can switch by backend if they want to.

items.description: Content block (text, image, document, …). Schema is intentionally permissive so new block types pass through.

AgentUserMessage.description: Inbound user-turn message. Forwarded verbatim to Claude's stream-json stdin; for Codex, text blocks are concatenated into a `prompt` string and non-text blocks are rejected (see spec §5.5).

NormalisedUsage.description: Per-turn token usage, normalised across backends. See docs/agent-events.md for the source-event mapping. additionalProperties:true so future fields backends emit pass through unchanged.

reasoning_output_tokens.description: Codex-only; surfaced separately (not folded into output_tokens) so clients can budget reasoning vs. text independently.

SessionSummary.description: One row in a blemeesd.sessions reply.
